figures.caption: Table 1. GEUVADIS prediction benchmark. The two prediction filters are CV-𝑟# ≥ 0.01 and held-out 𝑟 > 0.4. Paired comparisons are bsBSLMM minus each baseline on the shared retained-gene set.

Figure 2. GEUVADIS genome-wide prediction benchmark under both prediction filters (CV r² ≥ 0.01 and held-out r > 0.4), matching the convention used in Table 1. Left: per-chromosome counts of genes passing both filters for each of bsBSLMM, BSLMM, LASSO, BLUP, TIGAR EN, and TIGAR DPR. Right: per-method mean held-out test r computed over each method’s own set of genes passing both filters.

Figure 4. Functional enrichment of method-active cis-SNPs in external regulatory annotations. bsBSLMM active SNPs show the strongest enrichment in ENCODE GM12878 DNase and H3K27ac peaks, while TIGAR EN is strongest on GTEx v8 LCL cis-eQTL replication.

Figure 3. Posterior architecture and TSS-prior diagnostics across 23,098 GEUVADIS fits. A: joint distribution of cis-h² (PVE) and sparse share (PGE) — horizontal dashed line at PGE = 0.5 separates sparse-dominant from polygenic-dominant genes. B: posterior PVE vs 5-fold CV r²; the y = x line is the noise-free upper bound. C: posterior mean # active blocks vs CV r. D: histogram of the learned TSSdistance coefficient 𝛽<)*+, , with a median (solid) and the neutral 𝛽)*+, = 0 (dashed). E: pooled |𝛽<| vs |SNP − TSS| for credibly active SNPs in the top-500 strong-signal genes; darker bins indicate higher local point density. F: per-gene |𝛽<)*+,| (TSS-prior strength) vs bsBSLMM-vs-BSLMM held-out Δr, with the binned median in red.

Figure 1. bsBSLMM model architecture. Contiguous cis-SNPs are partitioned into LD blocks. A blocklevel spike-and-slab indicator 𝑧! gates each block, and active blocks contain SNP-level indicators 𝛾" with inclusion probabilities 𝜋" = 𝜎(𝛼 + 𝜅𝑎") learned from SNP-to-TSS distance. The model additionally includes a dense cis-GRM background effect.
